description: Create a full-screen drag-and-drop battle bidding overlay component that replaces the current slider-based bidding UI. Displays 4 vertical tactic tiles (Seppuku, Take Hostage, Hire Ronin, Imperial Poets) arranged horizontally, a coin pool with draggable coin images (one per coin the player has), and a confirm button. Coins can be dragged from the pool onto tactic tiles and back.
steps: Create a new component file src/components/BattleBiddingOverlay.tsx that implements the full-screen bidding overlay

The component receives props: playerName, playerClanColor, maxCoins (number of coins), onConfirm callback that passes {seppuku: N, 'take-hostage': N, 'hire-ronin': N, 'imperial-poets': N}, and battle info (province name, battle number)

Display 4 tactic tiles arranged horizontally left-to-right in order: Seppuku (切腹), Take Hostage (人質), Hire Ronin (浪人), Imperial Poets (詩人). Each tile should be tall/vertical with the kanji prominently displayed and a thematic visual representation (using CSS art/unicode since no tactic images exist)
Above/below the tactic tiles: a coin pool area showing individual coin images (import from src/img/coin.png), one image per coin the player has

Implement drag-and-drop using HTML5 drag-and-drop API (onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop). Each coin gets a unique ID. Track coin positions in local state: Record<string, string> mapping coinId to tacticId or 'pool'
Coins dropped on a tactic tile stay visually inside that tile. Allow dragging coins back to the pool to reassign them
Add a 'Confirmar Apuestas' button at the bottom that derives bid values by counting coins per tactic and calls the onConfirm callback

In BattlePanel.tsx, replace the hotseat bidding section (where effectiveBattleStepPhase === 'bidding') with the new BattleBiddingOverlay component, passing the appropriate props and calling doSubmitWarTacticBids on confirm
Also replace the online mode bidding section (isPart && !hasBid) with the same BattleBiddingOverlay component

Add CSS styles for the overlay in src/App.css: full-screen overlay with dark semi-transparent background, tactic tiles with Japanese aesthetic (dark backgrounds, gold borders, vertical layout), coin pool styling, coin images sized ~32-40px, hover/drag states, and responsive layout
Add any needed i18n keys to src/i18n/en.ts and src/i18n/es.ts (e.g., 'battle.confirmBidsBtn' for the button text if different from existing)
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no type errors
BattleBiddingOverlay.tsx exists and exports a React component
BattlePanel.tsx imports and renders BattleBiddingOverlay instead of slider-based bidding for both hotseat and online modes
The overlay shows 4 tactic tiles with kanji (切腹, 人質, 浪人, 詩人) arranged horizontally
Coin images from src/img/coin.png are rendered in a pool area, one per coin the player has
HTML5 drag-and-drop is implemented: coins can be dragged from pool to tactic tiles and back
Dropped coins visually remain in the tile where they were dropped
The confirm button calls doSubmitWarTacticBids with the correct bid distribution derived from coin positions
